summary: Full frontend UI testing of Breath Tower Rise Builder. All core flows work except the Welcome tutorial screen which has a critical bug preventing navigation through slides. Tested: welcome screen, home screen, technique selection, session flow (begin→cycles→end), summary stats, tower screen, settings screen.

ui_bugs.issue: FlatList programmatic scrollToIndex does not trigger onScroll handler in React Native Web. activeSlide state stays at 0, so button text never changes from 'Next →' to 'Start Breathing'. Users are stuck on tutorial - cannot complete onboarding.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): Welcome screen - FlatList onScroll not firing on programmatic scrollToIndex in React Native Web. Fix: Track active slide differently using onMomentumScrollEnd instead of/in addition to onScroll, or use a state-based approach to track which slide is active without relying on scroll events. Alternatively, add onMomentumScrollEnd={handleScroll} to the FlatList.
VERIFY: Welcome screen 'Start Breathing' button text change works and navigates to home after tutorial completion
critical_code_review_comments: welcome.tsx: FlatList uses `onScroll` to track activeSlide, but programmatic `scrollToIndex` does NOT fire onScroll in React Native Web. This breaks the tutorial flow entirely. Should use `onMomentumScrollEnd` or `onScrollEndDrag` which fire after programmatic scroll animations complete.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 breathing session during testing (2 cycles Equal Breathing)
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is mostly functional. After fixing the welcome screen bug, test the complete onboarding flow: 3 slides navigation + Start Breathing button. All other screens tested and working: home (floor count updates), technique selection (all 4 techniques + custom inputs), session (begin button, cycling, end session), summary (stats grid, build again/go home buttons), tower screen, settings screen.
rca_of_issue: Welcome screen RCA: FlatList.scrollToIndex() is a programmatic scroll that doesn't consistently fire onScroll event in React Native Web (Expo web preview). The handleScroll only updates activeSlide from user-initiated scroll events. Fix: Replace/supplement onScroll with onMomentumScrollEnd on the FlatList component. Example: add `onMomentumScrollEnd={handleScroll}` to the FlatList in welcome.tsx (line 80).
